title: 10k-message inbox with stream, throttled filter, and live insert
prompt: Write lib/my_app_web/live/inbox_live.ex (module MyAppWeb.InboxLive). Mount loads 10,000 existing messages from MyApp.Inbox.list_messages/0 into a stream (not assign). On connect (connected?/1), subscribe to Phoenix.PubSub topic "inbox:" <> user_id. handle_info({:new_message, msg}, socket) must stream_insert at position 0 so new messages appear at the top. Template has a filter input with phx-change="filter" phx-debounce="300" that updates @query assign; the stream is NOT re-assigned on filter (it stays full) — instead use phx-no-format + :for with a client-side data-hidden={not match?(@query, msg.body)} approach. Render with <div id="messages" phx-update="stream">. The stream DOM ID must be explicit via :id={dom_id}.
tier_rationale: Requires: stream vs assign choice, stream_insert position, connected? guard before subscribe, throttled phx-change, live filter without re-loading, phx-update=stream. Touches 4 capabilities simultaneously. Sonnet typically re-assigns the full list on filter (breaking the stream) or subscribes during disconnected mount.
research_citation: georgeguimaraes phoenix-thinking: 'Lists >100 items must use stream/3'; oliver-kriska: 'Always connected?/1 before subscribe'
